narrative_summary: The BRCA1 c.5165C>T (p.Ser1722Phe, S1722F) missense change occurs in the C-terminal region of the BRCA1 protein and has been reported in the context of hereditary breast‑ovarian cancer risk. One case report of an active‑duty servicewoman described the variant as pathogenic and noted that she underwent risk‑reducing surgery based on this classification [PMC8246613]. In contrast, a clinical re‑classification study listed the same alteration as a variant of uncertain significance (VUS) in a patient with breast and endometrial cancer, indicating that at the time of reporting it lacked sufficient evidence for definitive pathogenicity [PMC10092710]. Additional surveys of BRCA1 mutation spectra in Mexican ovarian cancer patients and Brazilian breast cancer cohorts included the c.5165C>T change among many other variants but did not provide an explicit interpretation, merely cataloguing it alongside pathogenic and likely‑pathogenic alterations [PMC9207274][PMC8907244]. No functional assays or computational predictions were presented in the extracted material, and allele frequency data were not reported. The available clinical evidence therefore is discordant: one source treats the variant as pathogenic, while another classifies it as a VUS, and broader population studies offer no definitive classification. Given the limited and conflicting data, the current evidence does not allow a conclusive determination of pathogenicity for BRCA1 S1722F; the variant remains of uncertain clinical significance pending further functional or segregation studies.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article reports that a high‑risk active‑duty service woman carried the BRCA1 p.Ser1722Phe (S1722F) pathogenic mutation and underwent risk‑reducing surgery.
article_mentions.title: Cancer Previvors in an Active Duty Service Women Population: An Opportunity for Prevention and Increased Force Readiness

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reports that Mexican ovarian cancer patients carrying the BRCA1 founder deletion (Del ex9‑12) experience significantly longer progression‑free survival when treated with olaparib, compared with patients harboring other BRCA mutations.
article_mentions.title: Clinical Benefits of Olaparib in Mexican Ovarian Cancer Patients With Founder Mutation BRCA1-Del ex9-12

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article proposes a compensation program to address harms arising from non‑negligent reclassification of genetic variants, highlighting policy and ethical considerations.
article_mentions.title: How should we address the inevitable harms from non-negligent variant reclassification in predictive genetic testing?

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study surveyed germline variants in Brazilian breast cancer patients, reporting many pathogenic/likely‑pathogenic mutations, but the BRCA1 S1722F variant is only listed among numerous variants without specific interpretation or classification.
article_mentions.title: Detection of germline variants in Brazilian breast cancer patients using multigene panel testing
